source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A thin disk of radius $R$ and mass $M$ lying in the $xy$-plane has a point mass $m=5 M/4$ attached on its edge (as shown in Fig. 1.109). The moment of inertia of the disk about its center of mass is (the $z$-axis is out of the paper). The disk is constrained to rotate about the $y$-axis with angular velocity $\omega$ by pivots at $A$ and $B$. Describe the angular momentum about $A$ as a function of time and find the vector force applied at $B$ (ignore gravity).
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: Angular momentum in the lab frame: $L'_{x}=\frac{-5MR^{2}\omega}{4}\cos(\omega t)$, $L'_{y}=\frac{3MR^{2}\omega}{2}$, $L'_{z}=\frac{5MR^{2}\omega}{4}\sin(\omega t)$; Force at pivot $B$: magnitude $\frac{5MR\omega^{2}}{8}$, rotating with angular velocity $\omega$

candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: A thin disk of radius $R$ and mass $M$ lying in the $xy$-plane has a point mass $m=5 M/4$ attached on its edge (as shown in Fig. 1.109). The moment of inertia of the disk about its center of mass is (the $z$-axis is out of the paper). The disk is constrained to rotate about the $y$-axis with angular velocity $\omega$ by pivots at $A$ and $B$. Describe the angular momentum about $A$ as a function of time and find the vector force applied at $B$ (ignore gravity).
candidate_problem_record.raw_answer_text: Angular momentum in the lab frame: $L'_{x}=\frac{-5MR^{2}\omega}{4}\cos(\omega t)$, $L'_{y}=\frac{3MR^{2}\omega}{2}$, $L'_{z}=\frac{5MR^{2}\omega}{4}\sin(\omega t)$; Force at pivot $B$: magnitude $\frac{5MR\omega^{2}}{8}$, rotating with angular velocity $\omega$

metadata.reasoning_chain:
In the rotating frame attached to the disk, the moment of inertia tensor about $A$ is
$$I= rac{MR^{2}}{4} egin{pmatrix}10 & -5 & 0\ -5 & 6 & 0\ 0 & 0 & 16\end{pmatrix},$$
and with $\omega=(0,\omega,0)$ the angular momentum is
$$L=I\,\omega= rac{MR^{2}\omega}{4} egin{pmatrix}-5\ 6\ 0\end{pmatrix}.$$ 
Transforming to the laboratory frame using
$$x'=x\cos(\omega t)+z\sin(\omega t),\quad y'=y,\quad z'=-x\sin(\omega t)+z\cos(\omega t),$$
with transformation matrix
$$S= egin{pmatrix}\cos(\omega t)&0&\sin(\omega t)\ 0&1&0\ -\sin(\omega t)&0&\cos(\omega t)\end{pmatrix},$$
the angular momentum in the lab frame becomes
$$L'=SL= rac{MR^{2}\omega}{4} egin{pmatrix}-5\cos(\omega t)\ 6\ 5\sin(\omega t)\end{pmatrix},$$
so that
$$L'_{x}= rac{-5MR^{2}\omega}{4}\cos(\omega t),\quad L'_{y}= rac{3MR^{2}\omega}{2},\quad L'_{z}= rac{5MR^{2}\omega}{4}\sin(\omega t).$$ 
Since the $y$-axis is a principal axis for the disk and does not contribute to pivot forces, the force at pivot $B$ arises solely from the rotating mass point. In the rotating frame, the mass point experiences a centrifugal force of magnitude
$$ rac{5MR\omega^{2}}{4},$$
so that the reaction force at pivot $B$ is
$$ rac{5MR\omega^{2}}{8},$$
which in the laboratory frame rotates with angular velocity $\omega$.

variants.title: SeePhys 开放题
variants.rewritten_question_text: A thin disk of radius $R$ and mass $M$ lying in the $xy$-plane has a point mass $m=5M/4$ attached on its edge (as shown in Fig. 1.109). The moment of inertia of the disk about its center of mass is (the $z$-axis is out of the paper). The disk is constrained to rotate about the $y$-axis with angular velocity $\omega$ by pivots at $A$ and $B$. Describe the angular momentum about $A$ as a function of time and find the vector force applied at $B$ (ignore gravity).
variants.expected_answer: Angular momentum in the lab frame: $L'_x = -\frac{5MR^2\omega}{4}\cos(\omega t)$, $L'_y = \frac{3MR^2\omega}{2}$, $L'_z = \frac{5MR^2\omega}{4}\sin(\omega t)$; Force at pivot $B$: magnitude $\frac{5MR\omega^2}{8}$, rotating with angular velocity $\omega$.
